arguments.task: Visit the official Klaviyo developer site and gather evidence from first-party pages only. Find and open pages for API overview/reference, authentication methods, rate limits, pricing/access or account requirements, and any page about MCP/server/AI tools if available. Also check official Klaviyo GitHub organization or repos for MCP if linked from official pages. Return a concise structured summary with exact page titles and URLs used, plus key facts quoted or paraphrased from those pages.

raw_result.description: Klaviyo provides APIs for customer data, profiles, events, campaigns, flows, lists, segments, templates, and other marketing automation resources.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe private key authentication for server-side integrations and OAuth 2.0 for Klaviyo apps/integrations; MCP docs also describe using an API key for remote/local setup.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developer docs describe creating a free developer account and generating private API keys from account settings; OAuth app creation is documented in the public developer portal.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad marketing automation coverage, including profiles, events, campaigns, flows, templates, lists, segments, metrics, catalogs, webhooks, and related resources documented in the API reference and guides.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Klaviyo docs include a Klaviyo MCP Server page with setup instructions for remote-hosted and local use in clients like Claude and Cursor.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs describe burst and steady windows and endpoint-specific tiers XS, S, M, L, and XL. Docs also note OAuth apps use a shared quota across all installations of the app, while private-key integrations have limits tied to the individual account, and responses include RateLimit headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: First-party developer portal is extensive and current, with API overview/reference, auth guides, rate-limit guidance, and an official MCP server page. Public docs indicate self-serve developer onboarding and both user-authorized and server-side auth models.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The browser run gathered evidence for auth, rate limits, API breadth, self-serve onboarding, and MCP existence, but pricing/access classification as self_serve_free is inferred from the documented free developer account/get-started flow rather than a separately verified pricing page in this run. Exact breadth wording and whether all resources are REST-only should be spot-checked by a human if strict precision is required.
